items.title: Hockey icon Marie-Philip Poulin provides update about her health
items.summary_auto: Hockey icon and newly minted Walter Cup champion Marie-Philip Poulin will provide an update about her health at 10 a.m. ET.

items.title: N.W.T. Fire reports 'serious incident' involving wildfire plane near Fort Simpson
items.summary_auto: In a bulletin late Wednesday, the agency said the incident involved a "bird dog aircraft" that was working with an airtanker group.

items.title: Qatar player suspended 5 games after breaking leg of Canada's Koné
items.summary_auto: Qatar player Assim Madibo was given a five-match ban on Wednesday for the tackle that broke the leg of Canada midfielder Ismaël Koné at the World Cup. Madibo was issued a straight red card for the incident during Canada's 6-0 victory over Qatar in Vancouver last Thursday.

items.title: Canadian singer David Clayton-Thomas, who led Blood, Sweat & Tears to hits and Grammys, dead at 84
items.summary_auto: David Clayton-Thomas, the powerhouse Canadian singer who lifted American band Blood, Sweat & Tears to the heights of pop music success, including Grammy awards and one of the biggest-selling albums of its time, has died.

items.title: Could the history of Mars be set in stone? Meteorite discovery may offer clues to Red Planet's past
items.summary_auto: Garnet found in a 4.4-billion-year-old meteorite gives potential insight into the history, development and geological processes of Mars.

items.title: Incel violence and the Montreal shooting
items.summary_auto: After a shooting in Montreal left three people dead, police found a manifesto tied to the incel movement. Why is this online subculture driving young men to violence? And what can be done to stop it?

items.title: As U.S. tensions persist, buy-local movement remains strong in Nova Scotia
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump’s talk of Canada becoming the 51st state made Halifax’s Dale Darling stop buying American products. And she's far from alone, numbers show.

items.title: Headed to the ER? Here's why you could now wait more than 48 hours to be admitted
items.summary_auto: A report released Thursday by the Canadian Institute for Health Information found that limited emergency room capacity, difficulties in accessing standard care and the country's aging population are to blame for patients spending more than 48 hours waiting in ERs.

items.title: Mark Carney's plan to bulk-buy unsold Vancouver condos might be a bailout, but it doesn't have to be
items.summary_auto: People need affordable homes, and thousands of empty condos in British Columbia are waiting to be sold. The federal government has an idea to help with both: Prime Minister Mark Carney is partnering with the B.C. government to convert vacant condos into affordable housing units.

items.title: Why an ex-national chief wants to sue Canada over its secret Indigenous spying program
items.summary_auto: Ovide Mercredi says he intends to sue the federal government over its surveillance of Indigenous leaders. It's the latest response to a CBC News investigation into the RCMP's “Native extremism program" in the 1970s. But as the former Assembly of First Nations leader now learns, the spying didn't stop there.

items.title: 'Mayday to Ottawa': $400M carbon capture facility could be cancelled after changes to Alberta's carbon tax
items.summary_auto: A shovel-ready, $400-million proposed facility to convert landfill waste in Edmonton into electricity that took five years to develop, could now be cancelled after the recent carbon tax agreement between the Alberta and federal governments.

items.title: Battle over 5-figure pay hikes for federal judges is causing unease in legal circles
items.summary_auto: The federal government and more than 1,000 federally appointed judges are at odds over the best way to settle an ongoing salary dispute, raising concerns in both the legal and political community that the case could end up tarnishing the judiciary's reputation.

items.title: StubHub World Cup ticket cancellations prompt calls for investigations, legal action
items.summary_auto: StubHub customers who had World Cup tickets cancelled at the last minute are calling on lawyers, politicians and the U.S. Federal Trade Commission to investigate the company.

items.title: Ottawa said military would send sexual offence cases to civilian police. It retained nearly 70%
items.summary_auto: Military police failed to transfer almost 70 per cent of sexual offence cases over the past five years to civilian police, despite the government committing to doing so as it worked to pass a permanent change into law.

items.title: Search for survivors underway, at least 164 dead after Venezuela struck by back-to-back earthquakes
Swiss collector eager to sell his collection of Indigenous artifacts back to communities
items.summary_auto: A Swiss collector who has amassed thousands of Indigenous artifacts says he is eager to have his collection repatriated back to the communities it has come from.

items.title: Strong back-to-back earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing at least 164 and injuring hundreds
items.summary_auto: Two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday, killing at least 32 people and injuring 700 more as buildings collapsed in and around the capital Caracas, interim President Delcy Rodriguez said.

items.title: Trump's D.C. reflecting pool is the butt of online jokes. What happened?
items.summary_auto: The pool's $14.7-million US renovation and subsequent rapid deterioration has become the butt of internet jokes. Some have dubbed June "ALGAEBTQ Pride Month" or commented that Trump, who promised to "drain the swamp" in Washington, has instead created a swamp.

items.title: Halifax-bound Air Canada flight diverted after captain became incapacitated, restrained by passengers
items.summary_auto: Flight 7664 was en route from Newark to Halifax on Wednesday afternoon. The captain experienced a medical issue during the flight and it was diverted to Boston. Air Canada says it is working to help its 61 passengers who were on the plane make new travel arrangements.

items.title: Bosnia-Herzegovina boosts chances of advancing at World Cup with win over Qatar
items.summary_auto:
Bosnia-Herzegovina eliminated Qatar and increased its chances of advancing at the World Cup with a 3-1 victory
in a Group B match on Wednesday in Seattle.

items.title: You asked, we answer: Canada's new social media rules for kids
items.summary_auto: The federal government is moving to restrict social media access for Canadians under the age of 16. Parents, educators and experts have differing opinions on the Safe Social Media Act — and plenty of questions about the details. So you asked, and we answered.

items.title: Canada finishes 2nd in Group B after 2-1 loss to Switzerland, will play South Africa in knockout game Sunday
items.summary_auto: Canada’s men lost to Switzerland 2-1 in a sweltering B.C. Place on Wednesday afternoon, ending their hopes of continuing their historic World Cup campaign at home.

items.title: While focus remains on men's World Cup, clock starts on 2027 Women's World Cup
items.summary_auto: While most of the soccer world is focused on the men at the World Cup, the countdown has begun for the 2027 Women's World Cup in Brazil.

items.title: Meet the Calgarian behind Team Canada's World Cup 2026 jerseys
items.summary_auto: Team Canada's 2026 kit has been in the works since the last World Cup four years ago —  and it's the brain child of a Calgarian. As the lead designer at Nike for the national team's latest kit, Calgary-based Holly Gallacher was behind the designs of the national team's home and away jerseys.

items.title: Carney announcing decision on future of 24 Sussex in coming weeks: sources
items.summary_auto: Prime minister Mark Carney is expected in the coming weeks to announce the government's decision about the future of 24 Sussex Drive, sources tell CBC News.

items.title: Toronto gang war led police to a trove of evidence in Ryan Wedding cocaine case, court docs say
items.summary_auto: More than two years after the arrest of alleged hitman Malik Damion Cunningham in a Toronto suburb, new details about the circumstances of the police operation have emerged from unsealed warrant documents. Cunningham was purportedly hired, armed and trained by Ryan Wedding’s alleged drug ring.
